ALGORITHMS TO LIVE BY

The Complete Mastermind Workbook

10 Computer Science Patterns That Will Transform Your Business, Focus & Life

Mastermind Session: October 22, 2025 | 9:00 AM EST
Host: Edmund Bogen
REIGNation Mastermind Series
"Because Random Effort is NOT an Option!"

A MESSAGE FROM EDMUND

Let me be crystal clear about something:

You didn't join this mastermind to collect more information.

You joined because you're tired of random results.

You're exhausted from making the same decisions over and over.

You're done with the emotional rollercoaster of "should I do this or that?"

You want patterns that work — not theories that sound good.

Here's the thing most business coaches won't tell you:

The reason you're inconsistent isn't because you lack discipline.

It's because you lack decision algorithms.

An algorithm is simply a step-by-step pattern that reliably produces a desired result.

Computers use them to sort billions of data points in seconds. Google uses them to serve you the right search result. Netflix uses them to recommend your next binge-watch.

And the most successful people I know?

They use them to run their businesses, structure their days, and make choices that compound over time.

I've spent 20+ years in luxury real estate. I've built multiple 7-figure businesses. I've coached hundreds of agents, entrepreneurs, and high-performers.

And I can tell you without hesitation:

The difference between consistent winners and inconsistent strugglers is patterns, not effort.

Most people work harder than necessary because they're solving the same problems from scratch every single day.

They're rewriting emails. Rethinking their schedule. Second-guessing their follow-up. Wondering if they should keep searching or commit to what's in front of them.

Meanwhile, the top 1% have installed decision algorithms that handle these choices automatically — freeing up their mental energy for the 20% of decisions that actually matter.

Today, we're going to install 10 of those algorithms into your business and life.

This workbook is your implementation guide.

It's not meant to sit on a shelf. It's meant to be written in, folded, highlighted, and used until the patterns become second nature.

The commitment I'm asking from you is simple:

Pick ONE algorithm from this session and install it this week.

Not all ten. Just one.

Master one pattern, see the results, then come back and add another.

That's how systems get built. That's how random effort becomes predictable success.

Let's get to work.

Edmund Bogen

Luxury Real Estate Broker & Mastermind Leader

Boca Raton, Florida

"Because Random Effort is NOT an Option!"

    SECTION 1: HOW TO USE THIS WORKBOOK

    This isn't a book you read. It's a system you install.

    Here's how this works:

    DURING THE MASTERMIND (9:00 AM EST, October 22, 2025):

    • Follow along with each algorithm as we cover it
    • Fill in the worksheets in real-time
    • Participate in the group exercise
    • Commit to ONE algorithm you'll implement this week

    AFTER THE MASTERMIND:

    • Review your chosen algorithm daily for 7 days
    • Complete the 30-Day Implementation Challenge
    • Track your results using the metrics provided
    • Report back to the group with wins and learnings

    THE GOLDEN RULE:

    Don't try to implement all 10 algorithms at once. Master one. See results. Add another. Repeat.

    SECTION 2: ALGORITHM #1 – OPTIMAL STOPPING (37% RULE)

    "Stop Searching. Start Committing."

    WHY THIS MATTERS:

    You're bleeding money and momentum every day you spend "looking for something better."

    Whether it's choosing a marketing vendor, selecting a listing to target, or deciding which CRM to use — the search itself has a cost.

    The 37% Rule (also called the Optimal Stopping Algorithm) is a mathematical formula that tells you exactly when to stop searching and commit to what's in front of you.

    Here's how it works:

    If you're evaluating options sequentially (you can't go back to earlier choices), you should look at the first 37% without committing, then choose the next option that's better than everything you've seen so far.

    Real-World ROI:

    Agents who implement this rule make decisions 60% faster while maintaining 95%+ satisfaction with their choices. That's months of momentum recovered per year.

    THE OLD WAY:

    • Look at 50+ marketing agencies before choosing
    • Second-guess every decision
    • Miss opportunities while "doing more research"
    • Suffer from analysis paralysis

    THE ALGORITHM WAY:

    • Define your total pool (how many options exist?)
    • Look at 37% without committing
    • Choose the next best thing you see
    • Execute with confidence

    STEP-BY-STEP WORKFLOW:

    Step 1: Define what you're choosing and your deadline

    • Example: "I need to select a video editor by Friday (5 days from now)"

    Step 2: Estimate how many viable options exist

    • Example: "There are approximately 20 qualified video editors I could hire"

    Step 3: Calculate 37% of that number

    • Example: 20 × 0.37 = 7.4 (round to 7)

    Step 4: Look at the first 7 options WITHOUT making a decision

    • Take notes on what you like/dislike
    • Identify your "baseline quality"

    Step 5: From option #8 onward, choose the first one that beats your baseline

    • Don't keep searching after you find it
    • Commit and move forward

    Step 6: Execute immediately

    • Stop second-guessing
    • Trust the algorithm

    Time Estimate: This process takes 2-3 days vs. 2-3 weeks of endless searching.

    PRACTICE EXERCISE: YOUR 37% DECISION

    What decision are you currently overthinking?

                                        

    How many viable options exist for this decision?

               (A)

    What is 37% of that number?

               × 0.37 =            (B)

    Your Algorithm Plan:

    "I will look at the first            (B) options without committing. Then I will choose the next option that beats my baseline quality."

    Baseline Quality Criteria (what makes an option "good enough"):

      SECTION 3: ALGORITHM #2 – LEAD FOLLOW-UP SYSTEM

      "Consistency Beats Emotion Every Time."

      WHY THIS MATTERS:

      Most agents follow up based on how they "feel" about a lead.

      If someone seems interested, they chase hard. If someone seems cold, they give up after one try.

      This emotional approach leaves 80% of potential deals on the table.

      The Lead Follow-Up Algorithm removes emotion and installs a systematic 20-day sequence that converts 40% more leads than random outreach.

      Real-World ROI:

      An agent doing 10 leads per month with a 10% close rate makes $X. The same agent using this system with a 14% close rate makes 40% more revenue — that's an extra $50,000+ per year for luxury agents.

      THE 20-DAY FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCE:

      Day 1: Initial Contact (Phone Call)

      Day 3: Text Message (Value-Add)

      Day 7: Email (Market Update or Resource)

      Day 10: Phone Call (Check-In)

      Day 14: Text Message (Personal Touch)

      Day 17: Email (Case Study or Testimonial)

      Day 20: Final Phone Call (Direct Ask)

      After Day 20, move to monthly touchpoints until they engage or opt out.

      COPY-PASTE TEMPLATES:

      Day 1 Phone Call Script:

      Day 3 Text Message:

      Day 7 Email Subject: "3 Things Happening in [Area] Right Now"

      1. [Specific data point]
      2. [Trend or opportunity]
      3. [Action item or insight]

      Day 10 Phone Call Script:

      Day 14 Text Message:

      Day 17 Email Subject: "How [Client Name] Got Results Like Yours"

      Day 20 Final Call Script:

      YOUR FOLLOW-UP TRACKER:

      Lead NameDay 1Day 3Day 7Day 10Day 14Day 17Day 20Result

      How to Use: Check the box on each day you complete that touchpoint. Track the final result (Close, No, or Monthly).

      SECTION 4: ALGORITHM #3 – EXPLORE VS. EXPLOIT (80/20)

      "80% Proven, 20% Innovation."

      WHY THIS MATTERS:

      Every entrepreneur faces this tension: Do I stick with what's working (exploit) or try something new (explore)?

      Too much exploitation = stagnation.

      Too much exploration = chaos.

      The Explore vs. Exploit Algorithm gives you a mathematical framework: spend 80% of your resources on proven methods, 20% on experiments.

      Real-World ROI:

      This prevents "shiny object syndrome" while ensuring you don't get left behind. You stay competitive without losing stability.

      HOW TO APPLY IT:

      Weekly Time (40 hours):

      • hours = Proven activities (prospecting, closings, client service)
      • hours = Experiments (AI tools, new content, testing platforms)

      Monthly Marketing Budget ($5,000):

      • $4,000 = Proven channels (referrals, past client outreach, proven ads)
      • $1,000 = New experiments (TikTok ads, AI video, new CRM)

      Content Creation (10 posts/week):

      • posts = Proven formats (market updates, testimonials, listings)
      • posts = New formats (Reels, AI-generated graphics, podcasts)

      YOUR 80/20 PLANNING WORKSHEET:

      What are your PROVEN methods? (The 80%)

      2.                                                                                 
      3.                                                                                 

        4. What experiments do you want to test? (The 20%)

          SECTION 5: ALGORITHM #4 – SORTING & CACHING

          "Organize Once, Use Forever."

          WHY THIS MATTERS:

          Every time you write an email from scratch, you're wasting 10-15 minutes.

          Every time you recreate a social post, you're burning energy that could go toward revenue-generating work.

          The Sorting & Caching Algorithm says: Organize your best assets once, then reuse them infinitely.

          Real-World ROI:

          Building a template library saves 10+ hours per week. That's 520 hours per year = 13 full work weeks recovered.

          WHAT TO CACHE:

          Email Templates:

          • Lead response emails
          • Listing presentation follow-ups
          • Market update newsletters
          • Referral request emails

          Scripts:

          • Objection handlers
          • Discovery call frameworks
          • Listing appointment outlines
          • Negotiation responses

          Social Media Posts:

          • Market update templates
          • Testimonial formats
          • Open house announcements
          • Just listed/sold graphics

          AI Prompts:

          • Property description generator
          • Email rewrite prompts
          • Social caption creators
          • Market analysis prompts

          YOUR TEMPLATE LIBRARY STARTER KIT:

          Create folders for:

          1. Emails (Leads, Clients, Referrals)
          2. Scripts (Calls, Appointments, Objections)
          3. Social Media (Posts, Stories, Reels)
          4. AI Prompts (Writing, Analysis, Content)

            SECTION 6: ALGORITHM #5 – GAME THEORY

            "Anticipate the Move. Control the Outcome."

            WHY THIS MATTERS:

            Game Theory is the study of strategic decision-making. It teaches you to anticipate how others will respond to your actions — then design your strategy accordingly.

            In real estate, this means: framing your listing pitch so sellers see you as the obvious choice, structuring offers so you win in multiple scenarios, and crafting marketing that triggers the response you want.

            Real-World ROI:

            Strategic communicators win 30% more negotiations because they're thinking two moves ahead.

            GAME THEORY IN ACTION:

            Scenario 1: Multiple Offer Situation

            Most agents: Submit highest offer and hope.

            Game Theory: Submit offer with escalation clause + personal letter + proof of funds + flexible closing. Signal strength on multiple dimensions so seller feels secure choosing you even if you're not highest.

            Scenario 2: Listing Presentation

            Most agents: "Here's why I'm great."

            Game Theory: "Here's what happens if you choose the wrong agent" (paint the pain), then "Here's how I prevent that" (position as the safe choice).

            Scenario 3: Social Media Content

            Most agents: "Look at this property!"

            Game Theory: "Here's what buyers are missing about this market" (create curiosity gap), then "DM me for the insider perspective" (trigger action).

            YOUR GAME THEORY WORKSHEET:

            What negotiation or decision are you facing this week?

                                                

            What does the other party want most?

                                                

            What are their likely responses to your standard approach?

                                                

            How can you reframe your approach to make your desired outcome THEIR best option?

                                                

                                                

            SECTION 7: ALGORITHM #6 – OVERFITTING / RELAXATION

            "85% Perfect Is 100% Launched."

            WHY THIS MATTERS:

            Perfectionists lose. Not because their work isn't good — but because they ship too slowly.

            The Overfitting/Relaxation Algorithm teaches: There's a point where additional refinement produces diminishing returns.

            After 85% quality, you're usually just wasting time.

            Real-World ROI:

            Launching 2 weeks earlier = 2 weeks of revenue, feedback, and iteration. Perfectionism doesn't just delay success — it prevents learning.

            THE 85% RULE:

            Email campaigns: 85% = clear message, no typos, working links. Don't spend 3 hours perfecting fonts.

            Social posts: 85% = valuable content, decent visual, posted consistently. Don't agonize over every word.

            Video content: 85% = good audio, clear message, authentic delivery. Don't reshoot 47 times for "perfect" lighting.

            Websites: 85% = functional, clear value prop, easy navigation. Don't delay launch for pixel-perfect design.

            YOUR LAUNCH VS. PERFECT CALCULATOR:

            Project I'm Currently Perfecting:

                                                

            Current Quality Level (%):           

            What would 85% look like?

                                                

            What am I obsessing over that doesn't matter?

                                                

            New Launch Date:                     ____

            What I'll learn from launching now instead of waiting:

                                                

            SECTION 8: ALGORITHM #7 – SCHEDULING

            "Freedom Through Structure."

            WHY THIS MATTERS:

            Most people think freedom comes from flexibility. It doesn't.

            Freedom comes from not having to decide what to do next.

            The Scheduling Algorithm blocks your day into repeating patterns so your brain can focus on execution, not decision-making.

            Real-World ROI:

            Time-blocked schedules produce 3x more output than "flexible" schedules because you eliminate decision fatigue.

            THE DAILY ALGORITHM:

            6:00-8:00 AM: Morning routine (workout, meditation, planning)

            8:00-10:00 AM: Deep work (prospecting, high-value calls)

            10:00-12:00 PM: Appointments (listings, showings, meetings)

            12:00-1:00 PM: Lunch + walk

            1:00-3:00 PM: Content creation (social, emails, marketing)

            3:00-5:00 PM: Admin (paperwork, follow-up, systems)

            5:00-6:00 PM: Review + next day planning

            6:00 PM+: Personal time (family, hobbies, rest)

            Key Principle: Similar tasks get batched. No random switching.

            YOUR IDEAL WEEK PLANNER:

            Time BlockMondayTuesdayWednesdayThursdayFriday
            6-8 AM
            8-10 AM
            10-12 PM
            12-1 PM
            1-3 PM
            3-5 PM
            5-6 PM

            Fill this out with YOUR repeating patterns. Then follow it for 30 days.

            SECTION 9: ALGORITHM #8 – MINDSET RESET LOOP

            "Pause. Breathe. Reaffirm. Act."

            WHY THIS MATTERS:

            High-pressure moments break most people.

            A deal falls through. A client ghosts. A competitor undercuts you.

            Your emotional reaction determines whether you spiral or respond strategically.

            The Mindset Reset Loop is a 4-step algorithm that intercepts emotional reactions and replaces them with strategic responses.

            Real-World ROI:

            Emotional control = consistent performance. Leaders who master this earn 50% more because they don't make fear-based decisions.

            THE 4-STEP LOOP:

            Step 1: PAUSE

            • Stop. Don't react immediately.
            • Take 10 seconds before responding.

            Step 2: BREATHE

            • Deep breath in (4 counts)
            • Hold (4 counts)
            • Exhale (4 counts)
            • Repeat 3 times

            Step 3: REAFFIRM

            • State your purpose: "I'm here to [serve clients/build wealth/solve problems]"
            • Choose your identity: "I am [calm/strategic/solution-focused]"

            Step 4: ACT

            • Ask: "What's the best move here?"
            • Execute with clarity

            MEMORIZE THIS SCRIPT:

            Practice this 5 times today. Out loud. So it's automatic when you need it.

            YOUR RESET TRIGGERS:

            What situations typically trigger emotional reactions for me?

            1.                                                                                 
            2.                                                                                 
            3.                                                                                 

              4. Next time this happens, I will use the 4-step loop:

              My Reaffirmation Statement:

              "I'm here to                     ______. I am                     ______."

              SECTION 10: ALGORITHM #9 – NETWORKING PATTERN

              "Relationships = Revenue."

              WHY THIS MATTERS:

              Inconsistent networking produces inconsistent income.

              The top earners don't network when they "feel like it" — they run a systematic pattern.

              The Networking Algorithm: 2 lunches per week + 8 events per month + 3 handwritten notes per day.

              Real-World ROI:

              Agents following this pattern generate 3x more referrals than random networkers. That's an extra $100,000+ per year for many agents.

              THE PATTERN:

              Daily:

              • handwritten notes to past clients, referral partners, or new connections
              • social media engagements (comments, DMs, shares)

              Weekly:

              • in-person lunches or coffees with strategic contacts
              • group event (networking mixer, community event, charity function)

              Monthly:

              • total events/gatherings
              • "value bomb" (host a client event, send a gift, create an introduction)

              YOUR NETWORKING TRACKER:

              2.                               _ (Name) on           _____ (Day)

                  SECTION 11: ALGORITHM #10 – LIFE EXPLORE/EXPLOIT

                  "Grow While You Ground."

                  WHY THIS MATTERS:

                  The same 80/20 ratio applies to personal development.

                  Spend 80% of your time reinforcing routines that stabilize you (exercise, meditation, family time).

                  Spend 20% learning new skills that expand you (coding, languages, AI tools).

                  This prevents burnout while ensuring continuous growth.

                      You now have 10 algorithms that can transform how you make decisions, run your business, and structure your life.

                      But knowledge without implementation is just entertainment.
